title: Operating Systems Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of operating system concepts

questions.questionText: What is the main purpose of an operating system?
questions.explanation: The primary purpose of an operating system is to manage computer resources like CPU, memory, storage, and I/O devices.

questions.questionText: Which scheduling algorithm can lead to starvation?
questions.explanation: Priority scheduling can lead to starvation where low-priority processes may never get executed if high-priority processes keep arriving.

questions.questionText: What is a deadlock in operating systems?
options: Two or more processes waiting for each other indefinitely
A process consuming too much memory
questions.explanation: A deadlock occurs when two or more processes are blocked forever, waiting for each other to release resources.

questions.questionText: Which memory management technique divides memory into fixed-sized blocks?
questions.explanation: Paging divides both physical memory and logical memory into fixed-sized blocks called pages and frames respectively.

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of a system call?
options: To request services from the operating system
questions.explanation: System calls provide an interface for programs to request services from the operating system kernel.

questions.questionText: Which of the following is NOT a condition for deadlock?
questions.explanation: No Preemption (not Preemption) is one of the four necessary conditions for deadlock. The others are Mutual Exclusion, Hold and Wait, and Circular Wait.

questions.questionText: What is thrashing in memory management?
questions.explanation: Thrashing occurs when the system spends more time paging than executing actual processes due to insufficient physical memory.

questions.questionText: Which IPC mechanism is fastest for communication between processes on the same machine?
questions.explanation: Shared memory is the fastest IPC mechanism as it allows processes to directly access the same memory region without copying data.

questions.questionText: What is the main advantage of multithreading?
questions.explanation: Multithreading allows multiple threads to run concurrently, improving parallelism and system responsiveness.

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of a page table?
options: To map virtual addresses to physical addresses
questions.explanation: A page table maps virtual (logical) addresses used by processes to physical addresses in memory.
